name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 75 of Hormuz blockade. **BRENT $106.29 MAY 13 (-1.4% 1D, +12.1% 1M, +56.9% vs Feb 28 pre-crisis ~$73); intraday May 13 print $107.05 implies a ~$107.77 May 12 close = Brent rallied another ~2% into May 12 close on Trump-rejection news + April CPI energy print + Aramco '2-5 ships/day' + UAE-Saudi-coordinated strike disclosure, then retraced May 13.** WTI $100.75 (-1.4% 1D). **THE $108-110 TRIGGER ZONE WAS TESTED AND FAILED — Brent settled at $106.29, BACK BELOW the threshold added to the graph May 12. StanChart $130-145 path therefore remains DORMANT.** The trinary band ($100-110) has now held through FIVE rounds of escalation (Trump rejection → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room weighing → Litani crossing → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes disclosure) = structural durability, not a transient. Drivers of the retracement: (i) Trump-in-Beijing structural ceiling = summit makes kinetic strike order operationally impossible May 14-16; (ii) EIA cut 2026 Brent forecast to $94.85 (TASS) — official forecaster moved DOWN, pulled away from Goldman's $85 toward JPM's $60; (iii) Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West routing + 'quiet limited Hormuz crude exports resumed' (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West pipeline + UAE bypasses + quiet Hormuz transits — explains why Brent has not gone parabolic despite headline 96% non-Iranian Hormuz collapse; (iv) Iran missile fire rate -92% per JPost + CNN = capability vs willingness gap publicly quantified. **A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS MAY 12) — three-year high; energy contributed >40% of headline gain (+17.9% YoY, gasoline +28.4% YoY)** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps); gold +0.8% only on real-yield channel competition. **GOLDMAN $85 vs JPM $60 = STILL $25/BBL DIVERGENCE; EIA $94.85 PULLED OFFICIAL-FORECASTER MEDIAN DOWN — spot $40+ above JPM's 2026 average means market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong or market in fear-premium regime that reverses on genuine de-escalation.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 'market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists past mid-June' (CNBC primary); Q1 +25% profit + East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity with Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd; ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8). **EIA also confirmed non-Iranian Hormuz crude exports avg ~500,000 bpd since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war (-96%).** PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED: physical (transit collapsed) signals extended disruption; paper-Brent hesitating at $108 threshold partly because of compensating Saudi/UAE non-Hormuz routing + Trump-Xi summit forced pause. Reversibility WITHIN BAND symmetric; asymmetric outside: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 path activates (cascade to inr-usd, nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance); downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend thesis re-emerges, CF/Nutrien fertilizer trade reverses violently. **TTF Gas ~€46/MWh range (May 11 last live print €46.23, flat-to-slight up into May 13 with no fresh European Council action); AWRP unchanged ~1% (8x pre-war); VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline (+1.0% 1D, +10.6% 1M, +28.6% 3M) — insurers + tanker market have NOT bought de-escalation.** Branch read remains: kinetic ~22-27%, extended-economic-warfare ~50-55%, negotiated-resolution ~18-23%. **TRINARY-REGIME HARD FLOOR ESTABLISHED AT $100-105 THROUGH FIVE SESSIONS OF NEWS ESCALATION.** Brent has been pushed up by: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE') + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz transit DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10) + Goldman Sachs raised 2026 average forecast to **$85/bbl from $77** (+$8) on extended-Hormuz-disruption thesis + VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn confidence-collapse signature + new OFAC sanctions wave (12 entities + 3 individuals, Iran→China oil) + second Kharg slick May 11-12 + UAE-Lavan revelation (April 8 strike confirmed retroactive). **GOLDMAN VS JPM $25/BBL 2026 AVERAGE GAP IS THE LARGEST BULGE-BRACKET DIVERGENCE OF CYCLE:** Goldman $85 (with Q2 trimmed to $90 on prior ceasefire optics now stale, Q4 base $71); JPM stays $60 (Natasha Kaneva — bearish base case, 'oil surplus visible in January, likely to persist,' expects voluntary + involuntary production cuts to clear surplus, argues markets are $10/bbl above fair value on Iran fear premium). Spot is $40+ above JPM's 2026 average — market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong OR market is in fear-premium regime that reverses on any genuine de-escalation. **TTF Gas €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline).** AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline; VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline +11.1% 1M / +32.6% 3M though Frontline -3.4% 1D today on Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply. **BRENT $108-110 BREAK WOULD ACTIVATE STANCHART $130-145 PATH.** Trump China summit May 14-15 forces pause window over 1-week clock close. If Trump returns from Beijing May 15-16 without ordering strikes, Brent could retrace $5-8 toward $97-100; if he orders strikes, Brent +$8-15 within 24h toward StanChart path. **PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED**: physical (Hormuz throughput, AWRP, VLCC rates) all signal extended disruption; paper futures still in oscillating trinary band reflecting Goldman-JPM divergence.

current.branch_status: Branch 1 (kinetic) ~22-27% (DOWN — Trump-in-Beijing forces 48h pause; Iran missile fire rate -92% caps near-term kinetic posture); Branch 2 (extended-economic-warfare/trinary regime) ~50-55% (UP — CPI confirms macro pass-through, sanctions architecture deepening, Iran state-media synchronised hardening); Branch 3 (de-escalation/diplomatic) ~18-23% (DOWN — Iran nuclear-file rhetoric escalation narrows landing zone).
current.regime_shift_marker: May 12-13 — TRINARY BAND $100-110 STRUCTURAL DURABILITY CONFIRMED THROUGH 5 ROUNDS OF ESCALATION. $108-110 trigger zone TESTED AND FAILED (May 13 $106.29 settle after intraday $107.05 print + implied $107.77 May 12 close). StanChart $130-145 path remains DORMANT. EIA cut 2026 forecast to $94.85 (TASS) = official forecaster moved DOWN, pulling away from Goldman's $85.
current.dated_vs_june_backwardation: Paper-physical bifurcation persists — physical (Hormuz throughput 2-5/day, AWRP ~1%, VLCC ~$100K/day) signals extended disruption; paper-Brent hesitating at $108 partly because of compensating Saudi/UAE non-Hormuz routing + Aramco Q1 +25% + quiet Hormuz resume + Trump-Xi summit forced pause.

active_triggers.failure_note: Brent broke above $105 on May 12 ($105.58) following Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal; peace-dividend thesis falsified for this window.

recent_signals.headline: BRENT $106.29 MAY 13 (-1.4% 1D, +12.1% 1M, +56.9% vs Feb 28 ~$73) AFTER INTRADAY $107.05 PRINT (implied May 12 close ~$107.77 = +2% rally on Trump-rejection + April CPI energy + Aramco 2-5 ships/day + Saudi-strikes disclosure). $108-110 TRIGGER ZONE TESTED AND FAILED — Brent BACK BELOW threshold added to graph May 12. StanChart $130-145 path remains DORMANT. WTI $100.75 (-1.4% 1D), spread ~$5.5. Drivers of retracement: Trump-in-Beijing summit-pause ceiling (May 14-16 = kinetic strike order operationally impossible); EIA cut 2026 forecast to $94.85 (TASS, official forecaster moving DOWN, away from Goldman $85); Aramco Q1 +25% on East-West rerouting + Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May + ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8); Iran missile fire rate -92% (JPost + CNN intel) = capability ≠ willingness ≠ logistics. **April CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS May 12, three-year high since May 2023, +0.6% MoM; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM; energy contributed >40% of headline gain, +17.9% YoY; gasoline +28.4% YoY; shelter +0.6%; BLS itself flagged inflation 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts')** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps). Trinary band $100-110 has now held through FIVE rounds of escalation (Trump rejection May 11 → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room weighing → Litani crossing → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes disclosure) = structural durability not transient. Spot $40+ above JPM's $60 2026 average = either JPM decisively wrong or market in fear-premium regime that reverses only on genuine de-escalation. Paper-physical bifurcation deepened: EIA confirmed non-Iranian Hormuz crude exports avg ~500,000 bpd since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war (-96%). VIX FELL to 17.99 (-2.1% 1D) — markets pricing Trump-in-Beijing = forced kinetic pause; gold +0.8% only (real-yield channel competing with safe-haven bid). Goldman-JPM $25/bbl 2026 average gap intact; EIA $94.85 pulled official-forecaster median DOWN. Reversibility: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 activates; downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend re-emerges.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-13 Section A + B + C2

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY (US, India, most W Europe markets CLOSED) — FRIDAY MAY 8 CLOSE $101.29-$101.73 STILL OPERATIVE. Two days of unsettled news flow accumulated against frozen prices: bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike (NEW — first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5; first inside Qatari EEZ; UKMTO Warning notice; NO claim of responsibility); CENTCOM precision tally 58 redirected + 4 disabled since April 13 (UPDATED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing); Qatar PM al-Thani Miami meeting Saturday + Axios scoop on Qatar-IRGC channel (UPDATED-MAJOR); Bahrain 41 IRGC-linked detentions (NEW); KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK (NEW — first hard-data signal blockade biting at production-export interface; CAUSE CONTESTED — Iran blames European tanker waste, Fox-quoted experts suggest storage-overflow dumping); UAE air defense engagement scale refined (UPDATED — 2 ballistic + 3 drones, 3 wounded). Monday May 11 Asian open is first repricing event. Expect Brent gap-up of $2-4 if no countervailing diplomatic news Sunday. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day baseline; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. Goldman Q2 $90 target stretched against zero-transit + 1,500-vessel data + Sunday Doha strike — likely revises higher Monday morning if trinary regime persists.
sources: Intel dossier Section A1, A2, A3, A5, A11, B1, B2, B4
UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)

recent_signals.headline: BRENT CLOSE $101.73 MAY 8 (+1.66% 1D); FRIDAY RANGE $96.07 → $103.96 = TRINARY-REGIME SIGNATURE OSCILLATION; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 3 INTACT BUT OSCILLATING. The Friday tape low → high → close sequence ($96 → $104 → $101) is the cleanest signature of a market not believing either binary side (deal signs OR full kinetic) and pricing the trinary middle. The $96 intraday low reflects MOU-progress hopes (May 7); the $104 May 8 intraday high reflects second day of US tanker strikes (M/T Sea Star III, M/T Sevda) + CENTCOM disclosure of 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech. Goldman Q2 $90 target now stretched against Lloyd's List zero-transit print May 7 + 1,500-vessel Persian Gulf standstill — likely revises higher if trinary regime extends past May 21. Standard Chartered $130-145 path remains conditional on MOU collapse. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 6-8x pre-war norm — insurers have NOT bought peace story; physical-paper bifurcation deepened.
sources: Goldman Sachs Q2 research note (institutional anchor)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED price tape multi-source ($101.73 settlement vs $101.29 yfinance snapshot — same signal); intraday range $96.07-$103.96 = CONFIRMED Friday tape; trinary-regime mechanism = CONFIRMED via concurrent CENTCOM blockade scale + Mokhber speech + Hormuz operational data

recent_signals.headline: BRENT COLLAPSED -7% MAY 6 ON AXIOS MOU SCOOP TO ~$103; TRADING $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING (+0.5% MODEST STABILIZATION); SUB-$115 CHAIN COMPLETED; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 1. World Bank April 28 forecasts Brent averaging $86/bbl in 2026 vs $69 in 2025 — 'largest energy price surge since 2022.' May 4 kinetic pop + pre-May-4 baseline both broken. 48-hour Iran response window closes ~May 8 evening — binary test for further leg down to $86-90 (MOU signs) vs +$8-15 bounce to $115+ (Iran rejects).

historical_summaries.summary: 11 signals. Oil surged faster than during any other conflict in recent history following US-Israel strikes on Iran and Iranian retaliation across Gulf s | Brent crossed $100/bbl for first time in four years as Hormuz closure entered second week with no resolution in sight | Crude topped $112 after Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields and drones struck two Kuwaiti refineries
45 signals. Brent crashed -14 | Brent continued sliding to $101 | Brent-WTI spread collapsed from $18-20 to ~$2-3 — market saying 'we no longer believe Gulf supply permanently disrupted
